channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump seeks to set aside hush money verdict hours after immunity ruling
item.description: In prior court filings, Trump contended he is immune from prosecution for conduct alleged to involve official acts during his tenure in office.
item.dc:creator: KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about the Supreme Court immunity ruling in Trump's 2020 election interference case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court's ruling Monday in former President Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case makes it all but certain that the Republican will not face trial in Washington ahead of the November election.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: After Supreme Court immunity ruling, Biden draws sharp contrast with Trump on obeying rule of law
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden warned Monday that a Supreme Court ruling granting presidents broad immunity from prosecution would make an unchecked Republican Donald Trump “more emboldened to do whatever he wants” if he regains the White House in November’s election.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hurricane Beryl razes southeast Caribbean as a record-breaking Category 4 storm
item.description: BRIDGETOWN, Barbados (AP) — Hurricane Beryl ripped off doors, windows and roofs in homes across the southeastern Caribbean on Monday after making landfall on the island of Carriacou as the earliest storm of Category 4 strength to form in the Atlantic, fueled by record warm waters.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO and KOFI JONES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Darrell Christian, former AP managing editor and sports editor, dies at 75
item.description: Darrell L. Christian, a former managing editor and sports editor of The Associated Press known for a demanding demeanor and insistence on excellence during more than four decades with the news agency, died Monday. He was 75.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court rules ex-presidents have broad immunity, dimming chance of a pre-election Trump trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday ruled for the first time that former presidents have broad immunity from prosecution, extending the delay in the Washington criminal case against Donald Trump on charges he plotted to overturn his 2020 presidential election loss and all but ending prospe
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump seeks to set aside New York verdict hours after Supreme Court ruling
item.description: Donald Trump's lawyers on Monday asked the New York judge who presided over his hush money trial to set aside his conviction and delay his sentencing scheduled for later this month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Things to know about the Karen Read murder case, which has ended in a mistrial
item.description: DEDHAM, Mass. (AP) — A judge declared a mistrial Monday after jurors deadlocked in the case of Karen Read, a woman accused of killing her Boston police officer boyfriend by striking him with her SUV and leaving him in a snowstorm.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hunter Biden sues Fox News over miniseries
item.description: Hunter Biden has sued Fox News over a miniseries it published and then took down from its subscriber streaming service Fox Nation.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court rules ex-presidents have some immunity, sends Trump case back to lower court
item.description: In a historic 6-3 ruling, the justices said for the first time that former presidents have absolute immunity from prosecution for their official acts and no immunity for unofficial acts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Jamie Foxx shares more details about his 2023 medical scare
item.description: Jamie Foxx has shared some new details about the 2023 medical emergency that left him unable to walk.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: Who is Gavin Newsom? Top Democrat floated as Biden replacement
item.description: After Biden's shaky debate performance, Gov. Gavin Newsom is one of the top names for his possible replacement.
item.dc:creator: Urja Sinha, NewsNation

item.title: Man who confessed to killing 4 people in Maine, including his parents, sentenced to life in prison
item.description: WEST BATH, Maine (AP) — A man who confessed to killing his parents and two of their friends and wounding three people in a highway shooting pleaded guilty to murder and other charges on Monday, and a judge sentenced him to the maximum term of life in prison.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: MS-13 gang member wanted for murder in El Salvador arrested in NYC after entering US illegally
item.description: An MS-13 gang member wanted for two counts of murder in El Salvador was nabbed by immigration officers in New York City. He was found with a loaded handgun and marijuana.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oklahoma school chief: Bible should be mandatory in classrooms
item.description: Ryan Walters believes teaching the Bible within its historical context of influencing American history is essential.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: AI-based prison concept would complete sentences in just minutes
item.description: Convicts would be hooked up to machines and their brains flooded with artificial memories for what feels like years.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: BET apologizes to Usher for 'audio malfunction' during awards acceptance speech
item.description: Usher received a major honor during Sunday night's BET Awards at the Peacock Theater in Downtown Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: Costco CEO says this is the most important item the store sells
item.description: Costco warehouses seemingly have everything from the popular $1.50 hot dog combo and affordable gasoline to gold bars and electronics.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Darien Gap's closure could impact migrant passage into US
item.description: New Panama President José Raúl Mulino has vowed to close the Darien Gap to migrants but he will need U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Tracking the Tropics: Beryl continues to strengthen as it crosses Caribbean
item.description: The National Hurricane Center is tracking Hurricane Beryl, Tropical Storm Chris and a new tropical wave.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Brieskorn, WGN 9

item.title: Epstein grand jury records released, describe trafficker's network for 'grooming' underage girls
item.description: Unsealed Jeffrey Epstein grand jury transcripts reveal how pedophile financier groomed juvenile victims for sex trafficking in Palm Beach, Florida.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A media 'nervous breakdown'? Calls for Biden's withdrawal produce some extraordinary moments
item.description: President Biden's performance in last week's debate with Donald Trump led to some extraordinary media moments.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What we know about the fatal police shooting of a 13-year-old boy in upstate New York
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Police in upstate New York tackled and then shot a 13-year-old boy to death after he pointed what turned out to be a BB gun at officers during a foot chase.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court allows part of Biden student loan repayment plan to go forward
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal appeals court has allowed the U.S. Education Department to move ahead with a plan to lower monthly payments for millions of student loan borrowers, putting on hold a ruling last week by a lower court. The ruling from the 10th U.S.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How the hot water that fueled Hurricane Beryl foretells a scary storm season
item.description: Hurricane Beryl’s explosive growth into an unprecedented early storm shows the literal hot water the Atlantic and Caribbean are in right now and the kind of season they can expect.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump lead over Biden unchanged since debate: Poll
item.description: Former President Trump’s lead over President Biden in national polling is unchanged following the debate between the candidates last week, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sotomayor's dissent: A president should not be a 'king above the law'
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In an unsparing dissent, Justice Sonia Sotomayor said the Supreme Court allowed a president to become a “king above the law” in its ruling that limited the scope of criminal charges against former President Donald Trump for his role in the Jan. 6, 2021 riot at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Supreme Court casts doubt on Florida and Texas laws to regulate social media platforms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday kept on hold efforts by Texas and Florida to limit how Facebook, TikTok, X, YouTube and other social media platforms regulate content posted by their users in a ruling that strongly defended the platforms' free speech rights.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former NFL QB, JaMarcus Russell, sued over $74k donation intended for alma mater
item.description: Former NFL No. 1 overall draft pick and LSU standout quarterback JaMarcus Russell is facing a lawsuit over a $74,000 check written as a donation for the football program at his alma mater, Williamson High School.
item.dc:creator: Simone Eli, Fox 5

item.title: Trump golf courses' liquor licenses not renewed after conviction
item.description: The licenses expired Sunday, but the two golf clubs were issued interim permits to serve alcohol until a hearing is held on the matter. The hearing is scheduled for July 19, which the division noted is after Trump’s sentencing on July 11.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Sforza, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Sneaky alligator caught hiding underneath Publix shopping carts
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) -- Yellow caution tape surrounded the front of a Publix store last week after a sneaky little alligator was caught hiding out underneath a corral of shopping carts.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: John Deere laying off hundreds of Midwest workers
item.description: John Deere said layoffs are being made due to reduced demand for products from factories in Illinois and Iowa.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, KRON4

item.title: ThunderShirts, dance parties and anxiety meds can help ease dogs' July Fourth dread
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Rori Chang will be spending Fourth of July watching action movies from the comfort of her Glendale, Arizona, home, perhaps something from Marvel or a John Wick flick. But it's not for her. It's for her golden retriever, Ava.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon surrenders to federal prison to serve 4-month sentence on contempt charges
item.description: DANBURY, Conn. (AP) — Longtime Trump ally Steve Bannon was taken into custody Monday after surrendering at a federal prison to begin a four-month sentence on contempt charges for defying a subpoena in the congressional investigation into the U.S. Capitol attack.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jamie Foxx reveals details about 2023 medical scare
item.description: Jamie Foxx has shared some new details about the 2023 medical emergency that left him unable to walk and kept him out of the public eye for several months.
item.dc:creator: TMX, KTLA 5

item.title: Michael J. Fox rocks out with Coldplay at Glastonbury
item.description: Fans at Glastonbury Festival were surprised by a performance by Marty McFly. On Saturday, Michael J. Fox joined Coldplay onstage during their set at the United Kingdom festival. The band's frontman Chris Martin welcomed the actor to the front of the stage in front of 100,000 fans.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Family demands accountability for NY police killing of 13-year-old boy. Police said he aimed BB gun
item.description: On Wednesday, Nyah Mway finished middle school in the central New York city where his family moved about a decade ago as refugees from Myanmar, relatives said.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, WGN 9
